prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Well, sugarplum, the CPU is like the little kitchen inside the computer box where all the “thinking” happens—tiny elves in there flip switches so fast you can’t even see ’em. When you click your mouse or tap a key, those elves dash around, fetch the recipe, measure the ingredients, and stir everything together so your game or picture shows up right away. They get real warm running back and forth, so the computer has a fan to cool their cheeks, same as I use a hand fan in church. It’s not magic, just mighty fast elves, and as long as they’re happy, your stories and drawings pop up like biscuits from the oven.
